cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out if the sentence "Bastian Schweinsteiger scored in added time" is plausible. Let's start by breaking down the components of this sentence. First, "Bastian Schweinsteiger" is a real person. He's a former German professional footballer who played as a midfielder for several clubs, including Bayern Munich and Manchester United, and also played for the German national team. He's known for his versatility on the field and has had a long and successful career. Next, "scored" means he scored a goal. In football, scoring a goal is a primary objective of the game. Players can score at various points in the game, including during regular time, added time, or even stoppage time. Then, "in added time." In football (soccer), added time (also known as stoppage time) is the time added to the end of each half to compensate for time lost due to injuries, substitutions, or other stoppages during the match.
